title: Python workspace defaults primitive migration

execplan_profile.projection_rule: canonical_core is authoritative for intent, scope, next action, proof, continuation, and closeout; legacy fields remain compatibility projections.

canonical_core.requested_outcome: Advance #892 by replacing the defaults selection and generic JSON output workspace facade delegates with contract-declared generated implementations.
canonical_core.hard_constraints: Keep command-generation package-agnostic; generated overrides must be declared in command_package_ir; do not claim full #892 completion while other facade delegates remain.
canonical_core.agent_may_decide: Generic override template details, validation tightening, and plan-local residue routing.
canonical_core.escalate_when: A correct fix requires broad workspace defaults payload generation, public behavior changes, or touching memory/planning runtime blockers.
canonical_core.next_action: Validate generated sectioned-payload selection and JSON output overrides for defaults/config generated operation paths.
proof_expectations: uv run pytest tests/test_workspace_defaults_cli.py tests/test_workspace_cli.py tests/test_workspace_cli_blackbox.py tests/test_generated_command_package_proof_runner.py -q
completion_criteria: defaults selection facade function is generated from an override template and no longer imports source runtime
generic JSON output is handled before source-runtime fallback
remaining delegates still block full #892 completion proof
selected generated and workspace proof passes

execution_run.what happened: Added contract-declared generated function overrides for defaults section/select behavior and generic JSON output, then rendered those implementations into generated/workspace/python/primitives/workspace_runtime.py.
execution_run.scope touched: command_package_ir, command package schema copies, command-generation renderer, generated workspace Python facade, generated workspace TypeScript metadata, and planning closeout
execution_run.validations run: uv run python scripts/generate/generate_command_packages.py --check; uv run python scripts/check/check_generated_command_packages.py; uv run python scripts/check/check_generated_command_packages.py --python-conformance; uv run python scripts/check/check_generated_command_packages.py --conformance --require-node; uv run python scripts/check/check_generated_command_packages.py --docker --require-docker; uv run python scripts/check/check_generated_command_packages.py --docker-conformance --require-docker; uv run python scripts/check/check_contract_tooling_surfaces.py --quiet-success; uv run python scripts/check/check_structured_file_inventory.py --quiet-success; make test-workspace; make lint-workspace; uv run pytest tests/test_workspace_defaults_cli.py tests/test_workspace_cli.py tests/test_workspace_cli_blackbox.py tests/test_generated_command_package_proof_runner.py -q; uv run pytest tests/test_workspace_cli.py -q; affected ruff

closure_check.accepted values: Accepted values: slice status = complete|completed|bounded slice complete; larger-intent status = closed|complete|completed for archive-and-close or open|partial|unfinished for archive-but-keep-lane-open; closure decision = archive-and-close|archive-but-keep-lane-open. closure_check.why this decision is honest: The slice migrates two safe facade behaviors but #892 still has source-runtime delegates and memory/planning blockers.
closure_check.evidence carried forward: Proof suite passed; static proof still blocks full completion while remaining generated facades delegate to package-owned runtime.
closure_check.reopen trigger: defaults selection or JSON output regresses to unconditional source-runtime delegation, or completion is claimed while other delegates remain.
